name: MidWest iCrate Single Door Dog Crate, 42-Inch

specs.Size: 42"L×28"W×30"H — fits large dogs up to 90 lbs
specs.Door: Single front door with two-bolt latch
specs.Fold: Folds flat for storage or travel

whatWorks: The included divider panel makes the iCrate the best crate for puppies at this price — you start with a small section sized to the puppy and expand as they grow, rather than buying multiple crates or leaving a puppy in an oversized space that undermines house training. The divider is simple to reposition without tools.

Folding flat in under 30 seconds is genuinely useful for travel, apartment moves, or homes where the crate comes out only for specific situations. Unlike plastic travel crates that require full disassembly, the iCrate collapses into a flat panel and fits in a car trunk without dismantling.

The two-bolt latch system is more secure than single-bolt competitors at this price — dogs that have escaped from cheaper crates by nosing the latch typically cannot defeat the iCrate's dual-catch mechanism. This matters significantly for anxious dogs that push and test boundaries.

Heavy-gauge wire construction holds up to years of regular use without bending or warping, and the powder coat resists rust even in humid conditions. The MidWest warranty covers defects in materials and workmanship, and replacement panels are available — a product designed to last.
worthKnowing: The 42-inch size is the right fit for most large breeds — Labs, Goldens, German Shepherds — but very large breeds (Great Danes, Irish Wolfhounds) need the 48-inch model. Measure your dog's standing height and length before ordering.

Single-door configuration means crate placement is limited — the door opens only from the front, which can be awkward in rooms where traffic flows from the side or back. MidWest makes a double-door version that adds this flexibility for a small premium.

The plastic floor tray can crack under heavy dogs or very active crating. Several owners of 90+ lb dogs report tray cracking within six months. A replacement tray costs about $20 and is worth having on hand, or placing a rubber mat inside to distribute impact.
Some dogs manage to move the crate across smooth floors because the rubber feet don't grip aggressively. A crate mat or furniture pad under the legs prevents this on hardwood or tile.

commonComplaints.title: Floor tray cracked under my large dog…
commonComplaints.body: My 95-pound Rottweiler cracked the plastic floor tray within three months. The wire cage is rock solid but the tray quality doesn't match. Ordered a replacement tray and put a rubber mat in — works fine now but shouldn't have been necessary.

commonComplaints.title: Single door placement is limiting…
commonComplaints.body: I wanted to put it against a wall with side access and couldn't. The front-only door forced me to rearrange my living room around the crate rather than fitting the crate into the room naturally. Should have gotten the double-door version.

commonComplaints.title: Slides around on hardwood floors…
commonComplaints.body: Even with the rubber feet my dog can push it a foot across the floor in an evening. I had to put a mat under it. For a product with this many reviews I expected the no-slip to be better.

commonComplaints.title: Took longer to assemble than expected…
commonComplaints.body: The instructions weren't great and I bent one panel slightly trying to fit it before realizing I had a panel reversed. First setup took me 25 minutes — after that it folds fast, but the first time was frustrating.

verdict: The MidWest iCrate is the most practical dog crate at this price, with the right combination of security, portability, and puppy-growing room through the divider system. Tray durability is the main weakness for very large dogs, and single-door placement limits room flexibility. For most large-breed owners who need a reliable everyday crate that folds for travel and lasts years, the iCrate is the clear value choice.

realBuyers.quote: Used the divider for my lab puppy starting at 10 weeks and expanded it four times as he grew. One crate from puppy to adult — exactly what I needed.
My previous dog destroyed two cheaper crates by nosing the latch open. The double-bolt on this iCrate has held through 18 months of my escape-artist shepherd trying every day.
I fold it every weekend and set it up every Monday for the work week. It takes less than a minute each way. The fold-flat design genuinely works for our space constraints.
Four years with my Golden and this crate looks exactly as it did when I bought it. The wire hasn't bent, the coating hasn't peeled. Build quality is excellent for the price.

sources.title: MidWest iCrate Review — Dog Food Advisor

metaDescription: MidWest iCrate 42-inch review for Canada: heavy-gauge wire, dual-bolt latch, fold-flat, divider panel included. Honest on tray durability. ~$79 CAD.
introText: The MidWest iCrate 42-inch is the go-to wire dog crate for large breeds, featuring a removable divider panel for puppies, dual-bolt security latch, fold-flat design, and heavy-gauge wire construction. Available on Amazon.ca (ASIN B002QJBOO6) at around $79 CAD.
